strengths: Structured source table categorizing primary and secondary materials
Well-aligned and detailed research questions guiding inquiry
Effective use of survey data to inform project direction
Rich qualitative insights from interview excerpts
Clear explanation of creative thinking to address technical constraints
Editing process screenshots demonstrating practical ATL skill use
Critical thinking applied to refine narrative and title
Intentional aesthetic choices (e.g., nostalgic filter) to enhance impact
weaknesses: OPVL evaluation of the UNICEF report lacks specificity in describing how its length and detail limitations affect your project focus

strengths: Figure 1 mapping of project goals, skills and social justice connections
Clearly articulated learning goal tied to personal passion and Fairness and Development context
Clear, feasible product goal (short documentary and GoFundMe campaign)
Basic research plan diagram sequencing secondary and primary research stages
weaknesses: Action plan table lacks sequencing, assigned responsibilities and detailed resource lists
Success criteria are described but not specific or measurable (e.g., no viewer engagement targets or donation goals)
Key action plan summary is missing concrete steps and deadlines linked to success criteria

strengths: Insightful reflection on technical skills and empathy development
Learner profile reflection with specific examples of risk-taking and open-mindedness
Evaluation of clarity and functionality using peer feedback
Comprehensive KWL chart demonstrating metacognitive awareness
Data-driven evaluation of video length, pacing and impact
Identification of future learning goals and next steps
Self-evaluation of aesthetics using survey feedback and rubric criteria
weaknesses: âWhat have I learntâ section appears truncated and needs fuller examples and evidence

annotations.comment: The detailed action plan table is populated but missing critical details: explicit sequencing, responsible personnel, and resource lists to ensure feasibility.
Figure 1 effectively maps out your project goals, skills, and social justice connections. This visual aids planning by making your success criteria and resource needs explicit.

Your learning goal is clearly articulated, tied to personal passion and the global context of Fairness and Development, demonstrating a strong foundation for focused inquiry and measurable exploration.
You state a clear, feasible product goal (short documentary and GoFundMe campaign), aligning purpose and format early in your plan.
Your success criteria list important video elements but lack specific, measurable targets (e.g., viewer engagement metrics or donation goals) to gauge achievement.
The âKey for action Planâ summary lacks concrete steps and deadlines tied to your success criteria, reducing its utility for monitoring progress.
Your basic research plan diagram clearly sequences secondary and two stages of primary research, demonstrating thoughtful preparation to meet your learning goal.
Your OPVL evaluation of the UNICEF report outlines value but the limitations section is vague. Specify how length and level of detail affect your projectâs focus.
Your table of sources shows clear categorization of primary and secondary materials, demonstrating effective research skills and source evaluation.
Your detailed research questions in Figure 8 are well aligned with the learning goal and demonstrate how ATL research skills guide your inquiry.
You leverage survey data (Figure 9) to inform project direction, showing effective application of research skills and evidence-based decision making.
Your interview excerpt provides rich qualitative insights into field conditions, illustrating strong primary research application toward your learning goal.
You clearly explain how creative thinking skills addressed technical constraints (soft voices, limited equipment), demonstrating effective application of ATL Thinking skills in product development.
Your âWhat have I learntâ section is insightful but appears truncated. Expanding with complete examples and evidence will strengthen your evaluation of growth.
Screenshot of your editing process (Figure 12.1) highlights how you selected and synchronized music to enhance emotional impact, showing practical ATL skill usage.
You applied critical thinking to refine your narrative and title, âTwo sides of a coin,â linking aesthetics and message in a compelling, open-ended question.
Your reflection describes how the project expanded your technical and empathy skills, demonstrating a clear understanding of personal learning impact.
Your learner profile reflection illustrates growth as a risk taker and open-minded researcher, supported by specific project experiences.
Evaluating clarity and functionality using peer feedback (Function) demonstrates an insightful assessment of your productâs communicative effectiveness.
Applying a warm, nostalgic filter (Figure 12.2) demonstrates creative problem solving and intentional aesthetic choices to support your narrative impact.
The KWL chart captures your prior knowledge and identifies gaps, showing strong metacognitive awareness and readiness to guide future learning.
Your evaluation of video length, pacing, and impact (Size and Impact) using quantified survey data reflects robust, criterion-linked reflection.
Identifying future learning goals (What do I want to know?) shows strong reflection on next steps and demonstrates commitment to ongoing development.
Self-evaluation of aesthetics against survey feedback combines evidence with rubrics, showing thorough reflection on product quality.
